Dispatchers can now visualize and validate up to 100 technician routes in a single view – enabling more accurate daily planning, better route optimization, and faster decision-making in dynamic operating conditions. For additional insights, see here.

Expanded Route Visibility – View Up to 100 Technician Routes

 

Faster Work Closure – Group Checkout Across Multiple Service Calls

Technicians can now check out activities from multiple service calls in one step – reducing admin time, streamlining workflows on-site, and generating a single consolidated report for all completed work. Read more here. 

Group Checkout Across Multiple Service Calls

 

Business Rules

This release also brings new flexibility to business rule automation, improving scalability and simplifying configuration.

Smarter Automation – Trigger Business Rules from Multiple FSM Events

You can now trigger a single business rule from multiple FSM events – enabling faster implementation, scalable rule creation, and a cleaner configuration environment by eliminating redundant rules and simplifying automation logic. Learn more in the documentation here.

Trigger Business Rules from Multiple FSM Events

 

Integration

Integration remains a key focus in 2505, with new phase model support aligned to SAP Cloud ERP Private – further advancing SAP’s capabilities in asset-centric field service by seamlessly connecting maintenance planning with field service.

Phase Model Integration – Automate Phase/Subphase Transitions for Maintenance Orders

This release enables integration with the phase-based model in SAP S/4HANA Asset Management (as delivered in SAP Cloud ERP Private) automatically updating maintenance order phases and subphases based on activity assignment, unassignment, and workflow steps. This reduces manual effort, improves process control, and helps ensure accurate, timely synchronization between enterprise asset management and field service operations. Learn more here.

Note: this integration is using the SAP FSM Cloud connector.

Automate Phase/Subphase Transitions for Maintenance Orders

 

Final Thoughts

SAP Field Service Management 2505 continues to strengthen the field service value chain with innovations like Joule for dispatchers, configurable generative AI summaries, improved map and routing capabilities, and deeper integration with SAP Cloud ERP Private. These enhancements accelerate decision-making, drive greater automation, and simplify service execution across the entire field service value chain, while laying the groundwork for future Joule Agents to enable more intelligent, autonomous field service workflows. 

We encourage you to explore these new features, share your feedback, and help shape the future of SAP Field Service Management by submitting your influence requests here. And stay tuned – even more innovations are on the horizon in 2508.

Have thoughts or questions? We’d love to hear from you. Let us know which features you find most impactful or what you'd like to see next. For more insights, visit the SAP Field Service Management Community page or explore our SAP Field Service Management website. You can also watch the SAP Field Service Management 2505 Release Demo here and register for upcoming webinars to stay up to date.

entry.title: Evento em Curitiba reúne SAP e especialistas para discutir inovação, IA e Reforma Tributária no GROW
entry.content:
No próximo dia 13 de junho , Curitiba será palco de um dos encontros mais relevantes do ano para líderes de tecnologia, finanças e estratégia empresarial: o CVA SUMMIT 2025 .

Realizado na Arena Digital da PUC-PR , o evento reunirá grandes nomes da SAP e especialistas do mercado para debater o presente e o futuro dos negócios digitais no Brasil. A programação inclui temas que estão no centro das decisões das empresas brasileiras, como GROW with SAP , Inteligência Artificial aplicada à gestão , plataformas de inovação como o SAP BTP , e os impactos da Reforma Tributária na gestão fiscal .

Entre os palestrantes confirmados estão:

  • Eduardo Ferraz , Head Brasil do GROW com SAP

  • Christian Geronasso , CRO Global de Inteligência Artificial da SAP

  • Arthur Proença , Consultor de Soluções Digitais da SAP BTP

  • João Emílio , Executivo SAP LeanIX e Signavio

  • Luiz Fernando Albertin , Gerente Engenheiro de Vendas na Avalara

Mais do que um evento, o CVA SUMMIT é uma experiência imersiva , pensada para fornecer conexões de qualidade entre profissionais que desejam estar à frente das transformações do mercado. Com um ambiente tecnológico e inovador, o encontro oferece ainda coffee break, almoço estratégico e estacionamento incluído .

“Transformação, inovação e estratégia fiscal em um só lugar”,  esse é o espírito do CVA SUMMIT 2025. Um convite para refletir, aprender e se conectar com quem está fazendo a diferença na jornada digital das empresas brasileiras .

Arena Digital da PUC-PR – Curitiba/PR, dia 13 de junho – a partir das 8h30 | Evento exclusivo para líderes de TI, CFOs, CEOs, Controllers e tomadores de decisão. INSCREVA-SE: https://solucoes.cvaconsultoria.com.br/summit-2025-cva

entry.title: What’s New in SAP Build: Highlights from SAP Sapphire 2025
entry.content:
As the SAP Sapphire 2025 global keynote outlined, this year's focus was clear: AI, applications, and data. SAP Build continues to evolve in alignment with these themes, helping organizations efficiently build, automate, and extend their business processes and applications.

Here's a roundup of Sapphire's key SAP Build announcements and innovations to watch this year.

Joule Studio: Intelligent AI Agents and Skills Builder

Joule Studio Capabilities

SAP unveiled Joule Studio, a new environment within SAP Build that helps you create, deploy, and manage AI agents and custom skills. With Joule Studio, teams can enrich Joule with company-specific skills and intelligent agents that work across SAP and third-party systems. Joule Studio brings two powerful capabilities:

  • Joule Skills: Design modular, reusable skills that can be invoked during conversations. These skills can trigger actions, pull data, and orchestrate SAP Build components like workflows, automations, or apps.
  • AI Agents: Go beyond skills by building full-fledged AI agents that dynamically execute business tasks using reasoning capabilities. These agents use business context, data from SAP systems, and large language models to adapt to user needs.

Joule Studio will be available by the end of Q2 2025, with Agent Builder capabilities expected in Q4 2025.

Early adopters like Sovanta, an SAP partner, already use Joule Studio to enhance customer service. Sovanta significantly streamlined support processes and accelerated ticket resolution by integrating conversational skills with SAP systems.

Another early adopter, Pricefx - a global leader in cloud-based pricing software - is using Joule Studio to bring AI-driven intelligence into their solution. By developing an Agentic AI copilot, they aim to deliver contextual, business-aware guidance that enhances pricing decisions for their customers.

Interested in learning more? Register for the webcast: Meet Joule Studio: Drive Innovation with AI Agents & Joule Skills.

New AI Capabilities for Business Users and Developers

  • SAP Build Work Zone + Joule: Employees can now use Joule to find relevant apps, tasks, and documents faster. It can summarize or generate content based on your business data, saving time and boosting productivity.

Joule in SAP Build Work Zone

  • AI-Enhanced ABAP Development: Developers migrating to SAP Cloud ERP can use AI to convert custom ECC code into ABAP Cloud standards. This feature simplifies modernization efforts while ensuring code quality and compliance.

These capabilities are expected in Q3 2025.

 Expanded SAP Build Capabilities

SAP Build continues to grow as a unified platform with:

  • Extensibility Wizard for SAP Business Suite: A guided wizard that helps you build side-by-side extensions with the proper context, faster. SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition and SAP SuccessFactors support is planned for release in H2 of 2025.

Extensibility Wizard in SAP Business Suite

Watch Sapphire Customer Session: Extending SAP S/4HANA Cloud with SAP Build at OGE: Open Grid Europe used SAP Build to extend SAP S/4HANA Cloud—boosting onboarding efficiency by 50% and reducing invoicing time by 20%.

Watch Sapphire Solution Demo: Extend SAP S/4HANA Cloud: SAP Build application development and automation. Discover how to rapidly personalize and extend SAP S/4HANA Cloud while maintaining a clean core.

  • SAP Business Data Cloud Integration: Planned for Q4 2025, SAP Build will offer deep integration with SAP Business Data Cloud (BDC) via CAP. Developers can consume and expose data products using virtual tables in SAP HANA Cloud, accelerating the development of insight-rich apps while reducing manual integration work. This improves data consistency and supports scalable, compliant development.
  • Event-Driven Applications: SAP Build, in combination with SAP Integration Suite and CAP, will support event-based interactions across systems. General availability is expected in H2 2025.

Simplified Pricing and Packaging

  • SAP Build offers a unified pricing model that combines app development, automation, and workspaces under a single entitlement. This simplifies adoption, reduces licensing complexity, and allows teams to build and extend at their own pace. Learn more in this blog post.

Thanks for reading! Which SAP Build innovation are you most excited about? Let us know - and don't miss these key resources from SAP Sapphire 2025:

  • Watch Sapphire Global Keynote: Your Best. Made Real. No Matter What Lies Ahead
  • Read SAP Sapphire 2025 Innovation Guide
  • Visit the Session Catalog with On-Demand Sessions

entry.title: Understanding Joule - (Step #7) Joule for Developers
entry.content:
This blog is part of the series Understanding Joule in 8 Baby Steps Please check the previous blogs, before proceeding.

 #1 – Introduction to Joule

#2 How Joule Works

#3 Joule Architecture

#4 Joule Interaction Patterns

#5 Joule in SAP SuccessFactors

#6 Document Grounding in Joule

In this blog, we will learn about Joule for Developers

 

What is Joule for developers?

Joule for Developers is the umbrella term for all the design-time AI capabilities offered by SAP that help improve developer productivity and proficiency.

These AI capabilities are provided across SAP Build solutions, including ABAP.

 

A Glimpse on Joule for Developers

Let’s have a quick glimpse on the Joule for Developers

 

Joule for Developers – A Comprehensive Developer Assistance

Joule provides comprehensive developer assistance for all SAP development needs. It understands SAP development framework intricacies, anticipate developers’ needs, offer intelligent suggestions, automate repetitive, and mundane tasks like documentation and sample data generation.

It can enable developers across low-code, pro-code, and automation projects to be more productive, creative, and proficient in accelerating apps or extensions for business applications like SAP S/4HANA.

 

Key Capabilities of Joule for Developers

As shown in below image, these capabilities comprise of new and existing AI capabilities that are enabled by Joule within SAP Build Code, SAP Build Apps, SAP Build Process Automation and ABAP in SAP BTP and S/4HANA Public Cloud Edition.

All these capabilities help developers build faster, code smarter, and automate better.

 

Here are some of the key capabilities:

 

Application creation

Generate code, UI, data models, and sample data across SAP programming models for Java, JavaScript, and ABAP, using Joule.

Below image shows Joule capabilities in SAP Build Code to help generate a data model.

 

 

Code optimization

Refactor code, create unit tests, and generate code explanations, summarizations, and more with natural language queries and intuitive actions with Joule.

Below image shows Joule capabilities in ABAP development tools to explain a CDS view.

 

Process and workflow automation 

Generate automation workflows and business rules using natural language queries.

Below image shows Joule capabilities in SAP Build Process Automation to help explain process workflows.

 

How to get FREE access to Joule for Developers?

The first thing that comes to our mind after learning about any new solution is – How can I try it for free!

Here is the good news – SAP provides few free trials for you to try Joule for Developers capabilities.

You can get started with SAP Build Code using a 30-day basic trial. This trial version can be used to test AI-based code development to generate app logic, data models, and test scripts. There is no setup required.

For more information, check SAP Build Code Free Trial.

 

What's Next?

Blog #8 Joule for Consultants 

For more information on Joule for Developers, refer to:

  • Unlock new levels of productivity with AI in SAP Build
  • Joule speaks ABAP!
  • Joule for Developers, ABAP AI Capabilities

entry.title: My SAP Sapphire Orlando experience
entry.content:
Hello, I'm Brian Kyser, a solution advisor with over 20 years of experience in the technology sector. I work with customers to help understand their issues and map those issues back to the value that SAP can provide. I like to work with customers to help them shape their vision of their respective programs to attain the most value out of an SAP framework. I aim to share my insights on how SAP's comprehensive service solutions can optimize your service processes, increase customer satisfaction, and achieve operational efficiency. 

This year I was fortunate to speak at SAP Sapphire Orlando. My session “Revolutionize customer service with AI agents in SAP Service Cloud” was about  how to connect the SAP Service Cloud solution with the SAP ERP application to optimize data, workforce, and processes while leveraging AI at every stage from issue to resolution to boost productivity and outcomes. Discover how SAP Service Cloud automates customer service processes and accelerates resolutions, driving efficiency with AI agents.  

The goal for the presentation was to show a day in the life journey from customer interaction all the way to field service work being assigned and the fact that this could be achieved through one application. Below were the main topics I covered: 

SAP Service Cloud manages customer service from interaction to resolution. Key features include: 

  1. Omnichannel Communication: Meet customers on their preferred channels. 
  2. Agent Desktop: Handle inquiries efficiently from any channel. 
  3. Case Management: Resolve customer cases efficiently. 
  4. Collaboration: Enable teamwork within and outside customer service.      
  5. Service Order Creation: Ensure end-to-end resolution with native service orders.      
  6. Case Intelligence: Resolve issues faster with intelligent assistance.      
  7. Topic Analyzer: Identify trends in cases for improvements.                   
  8. Feedback Gathering: Gain insights to improve based on customer feedback.          
  9. Field service technician scheduled directly from Service Cloud  

In addition to the end to end journey, I also highlighted our AI Agents powered by Joule for CX:  

Case Classification Agent 

Purpose: Categorize and prioritize new tickets without historical data. Tools: Read case details, apply business rules, and update fields. 

Q&A Agent 

Purpose: Answer product-related questions in service cases. Tools: Identify questions, search product documentation, and suggest answers. 

Knowledge Creation Agent 

Purpose: Generate knowledge articles from closed cases. Tools: Analyze closed cases, extract key information, and create articles. 

Conclusion 

SAP optimizes service processes, enhancing customer satisfaction and operational efficiency. SAP Service Cloud, SAP S/4HANA Cloud, and SAP Field Service Management work together to streamline operations, ensuring teams can focus on customer outcomes. 

Intelligent agents like the Case Classification Agent, Q&A Agent, and Knowledge Creation Agent further enhance operations. 

Before ending this post, I would like to share with you my personal experience from SAP Sapphire Orlando. 

My SAP Sapphire session was a high-octane sprint! I quickly iterated through key slides before diving deep into a dynamic demo. We kicked off with AWS Connect, seamlessly orchestrating a customer handoff to a live agent, showcasing a robust Service Cloud-to-S/4HANA-to-Field Service Management pipeline. The entire 20-minute journey, Q&A included, was supercharged with AI, demonstrating its pervasive enhancement capabilities. It was truly validating to connect with customers who are not just excited, but actively seeking to integrate our cutting-edge generative AI tech into their operations ASAP. 

SAP Sapphire consistently delivers. It's an unparalleled nexus for engaging with diverse industry players, all converging on shared objectives yet navigating unique tech stacks to get there. My prime takeaway? AI and integration aren't just buzzwords; they're mission-critical imperatives for most enterprises. However, their true transformative power is only unleashed when underpinned by resilient, high-performance tech foundations. It's about optimizing for maximum value extraction in this rapidly evolving digital landscape. 

Please introduce yourself in the comment section and share what you do in the world of SAP. Also, I would like to invite @BrendanDelBiondo  to introduce himself. 

That’s me with the vest!  

 

Brian Kyser Demonstrating SAP Service Cloud Version 2

Brian Kyser Speaking at SAP Sapphire for his session

 

Brian Kyser sitting at the Service Cloud booth at SAP Sapphire

entry.title: Common issues in SAP Joule Activation in SAP S/4 HANA Private Cloud Edition
entry.content:
Common Issues in SAP Joule activation for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition

 This blog is continuation of our comprehensive setup blog. In last several months, we helped multiple customers in activating Joule for their S/4 HANA Private Cloud systems  and in this blog we will compile all the common issues we faced and it's potential fix and workaround. 

  1. Issue: Joule refused to connect after the launch from S/4 HANA Fiori launchpad.

 

Reason: Trusted domain configuration not done.

Fix:  Add the Fiori launchpad URL (Host details) in both IAS-> Tenant configuration and BTP Trust settings. Please note that the trusted domain might take some time to take effect. Please retry launching Joule after 15-20 minutes in private browsing window. 

  

Screenshots

    

 

 

  1. Issue: Error occurred “Digital Assistant not found”.

Reason: S/4 HANA system is not added in the global account system formation.

Fix: Delete the existing formation for joule in BTP global account. In case of any error in formation creation, create a ticket with the component CA-JOULE-PRV. 

Screenshots

 

 

  1. Issue: Content provider synchronization fails with unauthorized error.

Reason:

  1. The user credential is invalid.
  2. The user is not having enough roles.
  3. Cloud connector version is > 2.15 and principal type “None” or “Use system certificate checkbox is checked”

Fix:

  1. Check and ensure the user credential is correct in DT destination.
  2. Ensure the user has SAP_FLP_ADMIN and SAP_FLP_EXP_USER roles.
  3. In the cloud connector make sure that the principal type is selected as “X.509 certificate”

Screenshots

 

 

 

 

 

 

Solution screenshot in cloud connector:

 

 

  1. Issue: Editing in IPS transformation, adding additional parameter failed with unspecified error.

Reason: Source / Target transformation contains error.

Fix: Copy the latest transformation from https://help.sap.com/docs/JOULE/6189c8655c484916bb8eb767126a653a/a6cf317ecede4fa78c57be07219cee1a.html?version=CLOUD

 

Screenshots

 

 

  1. Issue: Transactional query fetches informational / navigational result.

Reason: It can be due to many issues which may include the following.

  1. User doesn’t have the required roles for the transactional queries.
  2. OData service required by the transactional scenario is not active.
  3. Check the content provider configuration.
  4. Issue with the principal propagation setup.

Fix:

  1. Check the business roles required by the given transactional queries from the SAP Note: https://me.sap.com/notes/3523238 and ensure the user has the required roles. In case using the custom roles, ensure the required semantic objects and action are available in the custom roles.
  2. Check the status of the OData services as given in the above note and ensure they are active.
  3. Additionally, you can check the runtime destination for dynamic data in the content provider has the PCE SSO destination.
  4. Verify the principal propagation setup and check the cloud connector log, ICM log to perform troubleshooting.

 

Screenshots

The below screenshot depicts the resolution 3.

 

  1. Issue: Joule icon is not showing up in S/4 HANA Fiori launchpad after all the configurations.

Reason:

  1. User is not having the role which contains the business catalogue for Joule plug-in.
  2. Cache issue.
  3. 3rd party cookie is blocked by the browser setting.
  4. Fiori customizing is causing conflict with configuration.

 

Fix:

  1. Ensure that the user has the “Single Role” which contains the Fiori business catalogue.
  2. Clear the browser cache. Also, we can try invalidating the server cache by executing the program: /UI2/INVALIDATE_GLOBAL_CACHES
  3. Allow the 3rd party cookie in the browser setting.
  4. Use T-code: /UI2/FLC to check the launchpad content first with the setting “Configuration” and if no error appeared then run it for “Customizing”.

Check the following SAP Help documentation for more information:

 https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_FIORI_OVERVIEW/47d8a2e60bf2442c8e0675961953eef6/21484a647f9f4563942da3e846b5cc33.html

 

Screenshots

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

 

  1. Issue: launching joule ends up in error “Corp IDP” refused to connect.

Reason: It can happen due to multiple reasons. The most common we have seen is, the S/4 HANA Fiori launchpad is directly connected to corporate IDP (Microsoft Azure in this example) without having SAP Cloud Identity service as proxy.

Fix: It’s recommended by SAP to always use the same Cloud identity services tenant in Fiori launchpad which has been used with the BTP for Joule.

Screenshots

 

 

  1. Issue: Error in adding the Build work zone and IAS in formation after running Joule booster.

Reason:  The Identity authentication is not enabled in SAP Build work zone.

Fix: Enable the Identity authentication in SAP Build work zone.

Screenshots

 

 

  1. Issue: Cloud connector is not able to connect subaccount with Cloud connector

     

 

Fix: Download the authentication data from sub account and upload it in cloud connector, it’ll connect.

Thanks to my colleagues in @Diganta_Mandal @nageshcaparthy and others in AI RIG, AI Factory and Product teams. 

We will update this blog as we face more common setup issues.

entry.title: From UI to AI: How Effectively Can Joule Replicate System Actions?Can Joule accomplish tasks faster?
entry.content:
Initial Assumption

At first, I was skeptical. My early interactions with Joule suggested that its behavior didn’t align precisely with traditional UI-based transactions. However, after experimenting with a few scenarios, I realized that Joule not only mirrored the UI flow but also simplified it—achieving the same outcomes with fewer steps and in less time.

Test Scenario: Changing an Employee's Location with an Effective Date

Traditional UI-Based Flow

To change an employee’s location through the standard UI, a manager would typically follow these steps:

  1. Log in as a Manager.
  2. Locate the direct report whose location needs updating.
  3. Navigate to Job Information → Take Action → Change Job Information.
  4. Select an Effective Date and choose the New Location from the dropdown.
  5. Click Save.

Joule-Based Flow

Now let’s see how the same task plays out through Joule:

  1. Open Joule.
  2. Ask, “Show my direct reports.”
  3. Select the desired employee.
  4. Instruct Joule, “Update location.”
  5. Joule prompts for the new location.
  6. Provide a value, e.g., “New York.”
  7. Joule identifies multiple valid locations and prompts for clarification.
  8. Select the correct location.
  9. Submit.

Result: Location is updated in the employee’s profile.

 

 

           

 

 

 

           

 

 

The Catch: What About the Effective Date?

On comparing both methods, I noticed a subtle but important difference.

In the chatbot approach, the system defaulted the effective date to the current date—without prompting me. This behavior is problematic in real-world scenarios, where managers often schedule changes for future dates.

So, what happens if I try to update the effective date afterward?

Unfortunately, Joule cannot update the effective date of a previously submitted record. This is due to system restrictions—chatbots typically lack permission to modify historical records.

Can we simply re-submit the same data with a future date?
Not quite. If the location remains unchanged between the original and new record, the system ignores the update due to redundancy.

Here’s the error message that illustrates this behavior (see screenshot):

 

Solution: Crafting an Effective Natural Language Query

To overcome this limitation, I tried submitting a more complete command:

"Update the location for Faith Marshall to New York with an effective date of July 2025."

What happened next surprised me:

  • Joule asked me to confirm the location (since "New York" maps to multiple valid options).
  • Once confirmed, Joule returned a summary for my review.
  • The summary included all the correct details, including the effective date.
  • I clicked Submit.

Result:
✔️ Record successfully updated in the system.
✔️ Effective date was correctly set.
✔️ Verified via the UI.

 

 

 

 

 

Final Thoughts

This test clearly demonstrates the potential of AI-powered chatbots like Joule to handle complex HCM transactions with ease—when prompted correctly. With the right natural language input, Joule not only mimics the UI behavior but streamlines it, improving both efficiency and user experience.

For organizations adopting digital assistants, the key takeaway is this:

“Train your users not just to use the chatbot—but to converse with it intelligently.”

This shift in approach can unlock significant productivity gains, reduce manual steps, and support self-service operations at scale.

This is just an example- the options to perform smarter tasks in shorter period of time can be accomplished by Joule for Success Factors HCM pages. To give more insight-:

UI-Based Flow: Manual Steps and Clicks

To perform this action via the UI, a manager typically goes through the following steps:

  1. Login as Manager (1 click: Login)
  2. Search for the employee (1–2 clicks depending on how search is configured)
  3. Go to Job Information (1 click)
  4. Click Take Action → Change Job Information (2 clicks)
  5. Select Effective Date (1 click)
  6. Open Location dropdown and choose new location (2 clicks)
  7. Save the changes (1 click)

Estimated total: 8–10 clicks
Time: ~1–2 minutes (depending on network speed and UI responsiveness)

Chatbot-Based Flow: Conversational Efficiency

  1. Open Joule (1 click)
  2. Ask to show direct reports (typed or selected)
  3. Select employee from list (1 click)
  4. Ask to update location (typed)
  5. Respond to prompt with new location (typed)
  6. Choose the right match from suggested locations (1 click)
  7. Review and confirm (1 click)

 Estimated total: 3–4 clicks, 3 messages typed

 Time: ~30–45 seconds

Summary Comparison Table

Action

UI-Based Flow

Chatbot (Joule)

Clicks

8–10

3–4

Typing

Minimal

Moderate (3 messages)

Time Taken

~1–2 minutes

~30–45 seconds

Effective Date Flexibility

Manual Input

Requires explicit mention

Error Risk

Medium (missed fields)

Low (guided prompts)

entry.title: Joule Setup for SAP Integrated Product Development
entry.content:
SAP Integrated Product Development (SAP IPD) is a cloud solution that enables you to digitally orchestrate your product development from design-to-operate, resulting in reduced time to market, higher R&D return on investment, higher margins, and reliable product launches. 

In the current released version, SAP IPD business users have conversational access to the SAP Integrated Product Development documentation on the SAP Help Portal.  

Joule is supported in the following SaaS applications (capabilities) for SAP Integrated Product Development. 

Core capabilities Supported Scope 

Use Joule in the following capabilities 

  • Collaboration 
  • Common Functions 
  • Configuration Management 
  • Engineering. Note: Joule is currently only supported by the apps for Test Management. 
  • Enterprise Product Structure 
  • Insights 
  • Intelligent Handover 
  • Product Data Integration 
  • Product Innovation Management 
  • Specification Management 
  • Visualization 
  • Formulation 

Supported Languages: Joule currently supports the following languages (see image 1 below) 

(Image -1)

Please refer to the link for the most up to date information on languages supported by Joule.  

It is also important to note that SAP Integrated Product Development can be activated in the data centers that are listed here, and when integrating the product with Joule, please note to validate the data centers supported by Joule here.  

*************************************************************************************************************************************************

This blog post is a series for Joule Activation: 

  • SAP Business AI – Overview for all !!! (Includes information on Joule Supported Data Centers, Different scenarios during Joule setup, etc..) 
  • Joule - Unified Setup: Bridging Simplicity and Performance (Demo Video to understand the Unified Setup, Joule basics & architecture, how to set up Joule, etc...)  
  • Joule for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition - A Comprehensive Setup Guide 
  • Joule Setup – End-to-End Setup Guide (for all Line of Business) Unified Approach 
  • Joule for SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition - Setup Guide 
  • SAP Digital Supply Chain 
  • Joule for SAP Integrated Business Planning (SAP IBP) – Setup Guide 
  • Joule – Getting Started with Document Grounding - setup guide 
  • Activate Joule with SAP Build Work Zone and SAP Mobile Start 
  • Analytics Insights in Joule – Setup Guide 
  • Joule in SAP Signavio Process Transformation Suite – Setup Guide 

Joule in SAP Integrated Product Development – Setup Guide - You are Here 

Blogs - Work in Progress (WIP): 

  • SAP SuccessFactors Joule Activation (Refer to SAP Discovery Center) 
  • SAP Digital Supply Chain (WIP) 
  • SAP Asset Performance Management 
  • SAP Product Lifecycle Management 
  • SAP Digital Manufacturing 
  • SAP Concur 
  • SAP Ariba 
  • and more to be added towards General Availability 

*************************************************************************************************************************************************

Joule for SAP Integrated Product Development (you here) 

This blog will uncover the steps to activate Joule in two different ways (1) create a Joule for SAP IPD as a standalone product (Fresh activation) (2) activate and add Joule for SAP IPD with an existing subaccount along with your other activated / current LoBs landscape. 

Let's explore how to activate Joule for SAP IPD.  

Scenario 

Activate SAP's generative AI assistant, Joule, for SAP Integrated Product Development (SAP IPD) and understand the key factors that business users should consider when integrating Joule with SAP IPD.  

Use Case:  

Conversational search of SAP Help Portal documentation for SAP IPD: Your business users can ask Joule questions about the SAP IPD capabilities and processes, and Joule gives them an answer based on the SAP IPD documentation from the SAP Help Portal. This feature will provide quick access to user enablement content, summarizing answers from product documentation. It will save customers time by eliminating the need to browse through long search results and multiple topics. 

Pre-requisites 

  • You have an entitlement for Joule in the same global account on SAP BTP, Cloud Foundry, as your entitlement for SAP Integrated Product Development. 
  • You have subscribed to the Common Functions SaaS application. 
  • Your identity provider uses the OpenID Connect protocol. If you currently use a SAML protocol, see this information about migrating: Migrating from SAML to OpenID Connect Protocol 
  • Ensure that the EPD Admin role template is assigned to the user/admin working on the setup. For more information, see Role Templates for Common Functions. 

Integration Process: 

If you are activating Joule for the first time in your SAP Integrated Product Development system, we refer to this as a standalone/single setup, as mentioned in “Section 1”.  

In the standalone setup process,  

  • You may decide to use the existing IPD-Subaccount to activate Joule (check the data centers support for Joule), or  
  • You may plan to use a new subaccount dedicated to Joule, or  
  • If you would like to “add IPD” to an existing subaccount where Joule is activated, which is a unified setup as described below in “Section 2”.  

What does Unified Setup mean? 

The unified setup of Joule with multiple SAP products allows business users to ask any question irrespective of the Joule client they are in. This approach helps avoid toggling between multiple applications, improves user efficiency, and delivers a more consistent, connected experience across all LoBs. 

Check your setup plan: 

(Image-2)

We hope you've selected one of the setup plans by now. Before we begin activating Joule for SAP IPD, let’s understand how it works. 

The image below explains how Joule works with your landscape integration: 

(Image-3)

Note: In the current release of Joule for IPD, we do not use the SAP Build Work Zone and Cloud Foundry services. This is planned for the next releases.   

Now, since you have a clear understanding of IPD and how Joule works now it is time for us to start the integration process:  

Let us have a look at the steps involved for the integration: 

*************************************************************************************************************************************************

Integrating Joule  

  • Step 1: Setup Plan:  
  • Section 1: Fresh Setup – no existing Joule setup exists 
  • Section 2: You have an existing Joule subaccount, and you want to enable Joule for IPD 
  • Step 2: Post-integration checks 
  • Step 3: Joule / AI scenarios for IPD 2025 Roadmap 
  • Step 4: Joule test scenarios 

“Note: Always refer to the SAP Help Page for SAP Integrated Product Development to refer to the most up to date information. 

 

Step 1: Setup Plan 

Section 1: Fresh Setup – no existing setup exists 

If you already have some experience with SAP BTP (Business Technology Platform), we request that you follow the steps in this blog - Joule Setup – End-to-End Setup Guide (for all Line of Business) Unified Approach in this blog, and please check Section 2 to check your SAP BTP Account for Joule Entitlements. 

If this is your first time using the SAP BTP platform and you're unsure how to proceed with the activation, don’t worry - we’ve got you covered. Just follow the steps below to activate Joule for SAP IPD easily. 

  • Create the Subaccount  
  • Set up Trust Configuration with SAP Cloud Identity Service 
  • Add Entitlements 
  • Run the Joule Booster and see Joule in Action 

Step (1): Create the Subaccount  

  • Check this blog and follow step No. – 5 (refer to image below) and ensure you validate the Joule supported data centers.  

(Image-4)

If you are looking at multiple LoB integrations with Joule in the future 

  • Check this blog and follow step No. – 4 (refer to image-5 below) 

(Image-5)

Step (2): Set up Trust Configuration with SAP Cloud Identity Service 

The complete setup process is documented in this blog. Step No. 7 explains the process. For simplification, we have the reference images below. 

This setup is done at your new subaccount. Navigate to Security -> click on Trust Configuration -> click on Establish Trust and follow the images below.  

(Image-6)

(Image-7)

  (Image-8)

Step (3): Add Entitlements 

The complete setup process is documented in this blog. Step No. 8 explains the process. For simplification, we have the reference images below. 

(Image-9)

This completes the prerequisite setup at the SAP BTP Subaccount.  

Step (4): Run the Joule Booster and see Joule in Action 

The complete setup process is documented in this blog. Step No. 10 explains the process. For simplification, we have the simplified steps and images below. 

 

  • Click on Global Account -> Select Booster -> Search for Joule -> Run the Booster 

(Image-10)

The system will validate the entitlements required. Once it’s green, you can click on Next 

  • Select the SAP BTP Subaccount and click on Next 

(Image-11)

  • Select Integrations, you will be able to see the options for SAP IPD, select it, and click on Next 

 

(Image-12)

 

(Image-13)

Once you select the IPD System, you can click on Next, review the selections, and click on Finish. The booster will take 5-9 minutes to activate Joule and set up the integrations with your IPD system. 

Joule will now return the success message after the integrations are completed. You can validate this setup by navigating to Systems and Formation, searching for the Joule formation that will be created for your setup, as shown below.  

(Image-14)

Now, we have completed the activation process. You can go to your IPD system and check for the Joule icon as shown below.  

 

(Image-15)

Congratulations on successfully setting up the SAP IPD Standalone system! 

Now, let’s walk through how to add SAP IPD to your existing subaccount alongside your other active Lines of Business (LoBs). 

Step 1: Setup Plan 

Section 2: You have an existing Joule subaccount, and you want to enable Joule for IPD. In this option, we will show you how to activate IPD in the context of an existing Joule formation. Within your SAP BTP Global Account, navigate to System Landscapes, click on Formations 

Search for the Joule formation you have already set up. In my case, “I am using one of the existing formations, which has multiple SAP products connected to one Joule instance, as shown below”. You can click on Include Systems.  

 

(Image-16)

In the new window, filter for SAP IPD and click on Next step.  

 

(Image-17)

Select the option “Enable Capability Deployment” and “Enable the Joule Icon in Integrated System” and click on Review.  

(Image-18)

Validate the selections and click on Include.  

 

(Image-19)

The process may take 5 to 8 minutes to complete, and you should be able to see the synchronizing option, as shown in the image below.  

(Image-20)

 

Once the status(synchronizing) turns green and Ready, we are good to use Joule with IPD capabilities. 

Congratulations on successfully setting up SAP IPD with the Unified Approach! 

Appendix:  

What is Planned ahead  

Keep an eye on some of the exciting AI scenarios that we have planned for the rest of the year. 

2509 (Q3’25) 

AI-assisted 2D hot spot creation  

  • Feature would be released as GA (2509). Tagging master data to a visual model in IPD is a manual cumbersome and time-consuming process. AI assisted hotspot creation will automatically tag the relevant business data to the 2D visual models, thereby improving efficiency and helping realize a true visual digital thread. 

AI-assisted document summary  

  • Feature currently available as “Cloud Feature Beta”, will be released as Embedded AI (without Joule in Q3’25). Subsequently, we plan to embed Joule to this scenario.  
  • Scenario will allow users to summarize complex engineering documents and provide a concise summary by using the power of LLM. This will provide benefits by saving time in manual reviews and data interpretation. 

AI-powered text generation for Innovation Management:  

  • Feature will be released as Embedded AI (without Joule in Q3’25). Subsequently, we plan to embed Joule to this scenario. This scenario will allow campaign managers and idea submitters to leverage AI-based generation of new ideas based on campaign details, thereby helping to sharpen the campaigns and generate ideas using tailored prompts thereby helping in faster flow of ideas and faster time to market for launching new products. 

AI-powered text generation for Requirements Management: 

  • Feature will be released as Embedded AI (without Joule in Q3’25). Subsequently, we plan to embed Joule to this scenario. This scenario will allow requirements engineers to leverage power of LLM thereby helping to improve the clarity, structure, and consistency of requirement specifications. It provides real-time grammar and language suggestions tailored to engineering content. This ensures that requirements are easier to write, review, and understand across technical and non-technical teams 

Features planned for 2512 (Q4’25) 

AI-assisted Product Specification Search (GA 2512) 

  • LLM powered specification search will take inputs in the natural language form and provide contextual results using the customer database (that may contain 100000-500000 specifications on an average). The system recognizes the content and builds filters based on the query and is also useful for people who may not even be familiar with the property itself that they want to search for. The functionality will help save information retrieval time by finding specific details about a product by searching in natural language, rather than navigating through complex databases or documentation. Additionally, by enabling quick access to relevant information, developers can spend more time on actual product development and less time searching for data, leading to faster project timelines. 

AI-assisted Product Recipe Search (GA 2512) 

  • Similar to Specification search, this use case would leverage the power of LLM and Joule to search for IPD Formulation customers searching for recipes in their database based on natural language prompts.  

AI-based natural language processing (NLP) to search for documents 

  • Feature would be released as Cloud Feature Beta AI-driven NLP system for document searches, enabling users to quickly find information using natural language queries Interpretation and processing of user queries to provide relevant search results 

Now, let’s explore the Joule test scenarios outlined below. 

  1. 4. Joule test scenarios Link: https://help.sap.com/docs/JOULE/82a14f108cfa4d4788244d81371e072b/2ff600db0ce74e3e9dda6d8d0d81d761.html?locale=en-US 

In a similar approach, you may use Joule with a unified experience in other SAP products that you have integrated in the same subaccount. 

If you have any questions about the setup, you can reach us at SAP_AI_RIG@sap.com  

Thank you for staying connected with our blog and successfully activating Joule for SAP IPD! 

We hope you’ve had an exciting experience exploring the power of Joule, your intelligent business companion. From insightful decisions to streamlined processes, we trust you’re already seeing the incredible value Joule brings to your day-to-day work. 

Here are smarter, faster, and more impactful outcomes powered by Joule! 🚀

entry.title: My AI Experience Sharing - Setting up Joule for the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition (C: Deinstall)
entry.content:
Introduction

Now Artificial Intelligence (AI) is on every professional’s mind. Everybody wants to be on the bandwagon.  I plan to write a series of blogs on this topic under a big umbrella “My AI Experience Sharing”.  This is the first mini-series: setting up Joule for the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition. 

I purposely delayed my action on setting up a Joule to wait for the dust settling down during the Early Adopter phase. The intention paid off but still won’t give me a free pass 😉. There are five credible references available as listed in the Reference section, but none of them give you a good explanation on Why, especially on What in a critical step with a readable picture when you desperately need a hint.  To help all the people like me, this blog will focus on explaining those steps, what to enter, and possible mistakes to avoid. 

Note 1: The content here is drawn from my direct experience from setting up Joule for the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition (more than five times and still counting). It might be true for other SAP solutions but only use that as a reference so that I don’t feel the pressure of entire SAP world on my shoulder 😁.

Note 2: Since there are a lot of topics to discuss, I divided the mini-series into Part A Preparation,  Part B Joule Installation and Part C Deinstallation. 

Note 3: The systems I used in this mini-series are:

  • BTP Global Account: 2024-09 3SL Account
  • BTP Joule Subaccount: PSCC-AMER-3SL-PRD
  • SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Production tenant G4X/100, http://myxxxxxxx.s4hana.cloud.sap/ 
  • Cloud Identity Services: Provisioned together with above BTP and Public Cloud tenants, xxxxxxxx.accounts.ondemand.com

Note 4: Considering some customers and consultants need to deinstall and reinstall Joule for many reasons like practicing the installation, correcting an unsuccessful installation, etc., I am adding a new blog Part C: Deinstallation here for your reference.

 

Thirteen Major Joule Deinstallation Steps

Deinstallation is relatively easy. I list these steps in the below figure so that you can cleanly remove all the components in three systems, namely the Business Technology Platform (BTP), the S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition (S/4) and the Cloud Identity Services (CIS).

Thirteen Steps to Remove Joule Installations

 

References

  1. Blog 1: Joule – End-to-End Setup Guide (for all Line of Business) Unified Approach
  2. Blog 2: Joule in SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition - Setup Guide
  3. Discovery Center 1: Establish a Unified Joule Instance
  4. Discovery Center 2: Activate Joule with SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition
  5. SAP Help: Joule onboarding guide
  6. My Blog: My AI Experience Sharing – Setting up Joule for the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition (Part A Preparation)
  7. My Blog: My AI Experience Sharing – Setting up Joule for the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition (Part B Joule Installation)
  8. My Blog: My AI Experience Sharing – Setting up Joule for the SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition (Part C Joule Deinstallation)

entry.title: Measuring AI Access to Knowledge to Eliminate Thousands of Hours of Wasted Time
entry.content:
Organizations and their users often seek AI access to knowledge but rarely understand its full implications.

As subject matter experts or content contributors, how many times do we have to manually click on multiple internal enterprise-wide platforms (or locations) to find the structured and unstructured information we need securely…NOW?  

What is the cost in terms of time and money?

Let’s step back and reflect!

How many in our organization are struggling to find the latest information, the digital assets of record, to do their job as it relates to every line of business?

There must be a better way.

In Deloitte’s Global Human Capital Trends study(1) they found that AI access to knowledge ranks in the top three issues influencing company success, yet only 9% of the organizations feel ready to address it.

Survey responses included:

  • Over 50% of surveyed workers found it difficult to locate required information, and 80% reported needing to recreate documents because they couldn’t locate them in their company’s network.
  • 71% of individuals who found information easy to access, perceived its value as above average, emphasizing the link between accessibility and perceived value.

If organizations are carrying out these tasks manually and at great cost, it’s time to consider a better way  
to bring out the best of AI and search in heterogeneous landscapes.

How Streaming Services are Analogous to AI Access to Knowledge?
We can all relate to watching our favorite TV show or movie.
 
Imagine if your streaming service could only give you basic channels, but did not allow you the freedom of choice and seamless integrations with Netflix, Disney+, Amazon Prime Video, Apple TV+, BBC iPlayer, and/or WOW (Sky Deutschland). This access is expected.

Clearly it is advantageous to connect to multiple apps and systems, pulling information from diverse sources.

❓Question: Would you want to be limited to the basic service only, without the technical capability to get the access to what you want and need?

This example highlights that whether we want access to our favorite streaming services, digital asset, or knowledge, it is important in both our professional and personal lives to pinpoint what we want, when we want it, regardless of where it is located for business or home use.

Beyond the technical capabilities, why move forward unless there is a clear return on investment (ROI).

The ROI Equation: Knowledge = Competitive Advantage
Organizations struggle to define and measure the exact impact of their knowledge access initiatives. 

Consider quantifying the ROI that applies to all lines of business including sales, marketing, communications, HR, onboarding, call centers, finance, operations, supply chain, suppliers, business networks, IT and product support.

Here is a simple equation…

        “X” Hours (to find information) x “Y” Users = “Z” Total Hours Spent Each Year

💡Suggestion: Assess your organization’s activities per line of business and calculate the math.

If there was a way to reduce the time and costs associated with this manual activity, would you be interested?

💡Suggestion: Use conservative numbers to equate the time, cost, and ROI for your organization?

If this ROI formula sounds hard to believe, consider how PepsiCo Saves 5000 Hours per Year with the Capacity Answer Engine.

The Capacity Answer Engine has a proven track record of saving their customers’ thousands of hours, which equates to dollars saved, by providing secure (ISO/IEC 27001 certified), AI access to knowledge in seconds versus hours (or never found manually in the first place).

This solution provides a library of 100+ connectors which require a huge investment to build, maintain and optimize using a federated search and indexing capability combined with Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) (with an emphasis in the “Retrieval”) to bring out the best of AI and search together. 

For example, Microsoft Copilot isn't designed to perform a federated search or index on internal company documents or digital assets directly. However, Capacity Answer Engine, which is part of the SAP Store and Microsoft Azure Marketplace, can provide knowledge to Copilot via Retrieval Augmented Generation (RAG) to become the cited bibliography for an organization's generative AI.

How AI Can Unlock Knowledge Across the SAP Ecosystem and Beyond
So how does this apply to the SAP ecosystem?     

⭐ When looking at the SAP Business Suite landscape combining AI, data, and applications with the SAP Business Technology Platform, let us consider one or more of the following: SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public or Private Edition, SAP Ariba Strategic Sourcing Suite, SAP Business Network, SAP Commerce Cloud, SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central, SAP Integrated Business Planning for Supply Chain, and others.

Each SAP product or solution has a plethora of digital assets found in multiple internal global locations (or sites) that are at the core of running the business.

Most organizations have heterogenous environments, which includes 3rd party applications, tools, platforms such as:

  • Document Storage (e.g., SharePoint, Dropbox, Google Drive, GitHub)
  • Systems (e.g., Jira, Slack)
  • Messaging Platforms (e.g., Zoom, Microsoft Teams, Copilot)  
  • Dashboards & People Search (e.g., Microsoft Power BI)
  • Publishers & Aggregators (e.g., Paid subscriptions, IDC, NielsenIQ)

Limits in the number of data sources and incomplete integration coverage can be problematic. Also, mixing internal with external searches may result in inaccurate, outdated, misinformation from questionable sources.

What is needed is the pre-built connectors from the Capacity Answer Engine library, which are maintained for ongoing performance, scalability, security, and continuously syncs as information is updated, leveraging ID management role-based access control, and ensuring that users have the appropriate access to information and systems based on their job roles and responsibilities.

Looking Ahead: AI Access to Knowledge Will Reshape What We Know—and How We Use It
With the constant changes of market conditions, facts, data, digital assets, products, solutions, services, regulations, compliance, and risks, it is crucial that employees have fast, secure access to the most accurate and up-to-date information.

Organizations that enable its workforce to leverage federated search and index navigation of the constant, overwhelming output of internal organization information (& intellectual properties) will have a higher rate of success with revenues, growth, operational efficiencies, and reducing costs.

Leaders, business and IT experts should consider:

  1. Recognizing the importance of the right information at the right time, anywhere in the organization.
  2. Embracing AI access to knowledge to empower the workforce today.
  3. Prioritizing rapid access to enterprise-wide digital assets as an accelerator, not a barrier.
  4. Optimizing next-generation tools such as the Capacity Answer Engine.

Once this Capacity Answer Engine solution is in place, organizations can consider reinvesting the cost savings (and better use of employees’ time) to shift towards higher priority, higher value activities.     

Many organizations are already seeing these tangible benefits with a growing importance going forward.

Where do you stand?  

*****************
Let’s explore this topic further—what questions, comments or ideas do you have?

  • How would you rate your organization on a scale of 1 to 10 (10 = Best).
  • Can you search multiple videos to find the specific topic or key word you’re looking for?
  • What are your thoughts on the AI access to knowledge in your organizations?
  • Does your organization have federated indexing for AI and Search capabilities?

Very much appreciate your insights! Happy to start a discussion per your posts below.  😊

---

(1) Source: Deloitte Insights:  The new knowledge management - The human factor activates the collective intelligence

Useful Links

  • AI-Powered Game Changer: Revolutionizing Access to Knowledge – LinkedIn | SAP Community
  • Capacity Answer Engine
  • SAP Store – Lucy AI by Capacity Answer Engine
  • Capacity Answer Engine pioneers next-gen enterprise search using Phi models from Microsoft
  • RAG vs ChatGPT  

-----
About the Author

Rich Blumberg is a long time SAP Community member and contributor. For 20+ years he's been an SAP consultant working with SAP ecosystem organizations around the globe. He's the President of World Sales Solutions, LLC, (WSS) a business development and low-code no-code services company. In his spare time, he is a Drexel University Alumni Board of Governors Emeritus volunteer, ENGin English tutor for Ukrainian citizens, and an aspiring guitar player.

entry.title: Understanding Joule - (Step #8) Joule for Consultants
entry.content:
This blog is part of the series Understanding Joule in 8 Baby Steps Please check the previous blogs, before proceeding.

 #1 – Introduction to Joule

#2 How Joule Works

#3 Joule Architecture

#4 Joule Interaction Patterns

#5 Joule in SAP SuccessFactors

#6 Document Grounding in Joule

#7 Joule for Developers

In this blog, we will learn about Joule for Consultants.

 

What is SAP Joule for Consultants?

SAP Joule for Consultants is an AI-powered assistant, which is designed to enhance the efficiency of consultants working on SAP projects.

It capability leverages the most up-to-date and comprehensive knowledge base to deliver concise and accurate answers for various SAP topics.

Unlike other flavours of Joule which are embedded inside SAP solutions, SAP Joule for Consultants is a SaaS offering and can be accessed directly from a browser.

 

How to access Joule for Consultants

Once customers buy the license for Joule for Consultants, they are provided it as a SaaS offering. Users can then access the Joule for Consultants using the dedicated URL provided by their organization.

Below image shows the landing page. Just start a conversation by typing your question in the input field.

 

 

Here are some of the example questions you can ask to Joule for Consultants:

  • What are the key configuration steps to activate India GST in SAP S/4HANA?
  • How to create a subaccount in SAP BTP?
  • Generate ABAP code for a sales price calculation BAdI that decreases the location-specific price by 10% for every 2 degrees above 20 Celsius.
  • Generate a sample configuration guide for setting up a new company code.
  • How can I optimize freight costs using advanced algorithms and real-time data in SAP Transportation Management?

Below image shows a response from Joule for Consultants.

 

Who are the targeted users for SAP Joule for Consultants?

SAP consultants working on various tasks such as code analysis, documentation lookup, troubleshooting, implementation etc. can use this Joule to reduce their effort.

SAP projects involve multiple systems, complex business processes, and hundreds of configurations and code artifacts. Traditionally, consultants spend a significant amount of time in:

  • Searching through SAP Help, Notes, and documentation
  • Interpreting ABAP code written by others
  • Debugging system issues without clear direction
  • Repeating tasks manually across systems

 

With SAP Joule for Consultants, they can simply ask their questions in plain English and get curated, accurate and context-aware response. It will save lot of time and effort.

 

How is SAP Joule for Consultants better that other AI assistants, such as ChatGPT?

General AI tools such as ChatGPT are very powerful for broad knowledge retrieval and conversational AI. However, they have some major issues in the context of SAP knowledge, for example:

  • They have access to only publicly available information.
  • They lack accurate, real-time access to latest content from SAP. Due to which they might provide outdated or generic responses.
  • They source information from various non-official and community-based inputs, making it less reliable for enterprise SAP consulting.
  • They operate as a standalone AI and cannot interact with SAP directly. This limits their effectiveness.
  • They lack enterprise-specific security features. This means organizations are responsible for putting the necessary safeguards in place.

 

SAP Joule for Consultants is designed to solve all these problems. Let’s see how!

 

Comprehensive Knowledge Base

SAP Joule for Consultants is built upon:

  • 200,000+ pages of SAP documentation and learning content
  • 50+ SAP Certifications
  • 2+ terabytes of expertly curated SAP Communities content

This vast knowledge base equates to knowledge that would typically require a decade of consultant training.

 

Largest SAP-specific code repository

SAP Joule for Consultants has access to:

  • 250 million lines of ABAP code
  • 30 million lines of CDS code
  • Concise summary of code purpose, logic, and structure

Therefore, it can provide explanations and guidance on ABAP code to help consultants understand code purpose, logic, and structure and many more.

 

Up-to-Date Information and High Precision

SAP Joule for Consultants delivers responses grounded in official SAP documentation and verified resources. It ensures the reliability and alignment with SAP standards.

It also is continuously updated with the latest SAP releases, best practices, and security updates. Hence can provide accurate response on latest topics.

SAP Joule for Consultants is aligned with SAP security, compliance, and governance guidelines.

 

How does SAP Joule for Consultants work?

SAP Joule for Consultants uses Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) to provide accurate and relevant answers. RAG combines two powerful techniques to ensure the information is both smart and up to date. First, it retrieves information from a large database or collection of documents to find the most relevant details related to the question. Then, it generates a detailed and accurate answer using the information found.

This way, SAP Joule for Consultants ensures that the answers are based on the latest and most reliable information available.

 

Hope by now, you have got a clear understanding on Joule for Consultants. For more information, you may refer to

  • SAP Joule for Consultants
  • SAP Joule for Consultants Help
  • SAP Joule for Consultants Pricing and Product details
  • KPMG and SAP Joule for Consultants: Pioneering the Future of AI-Driven Consulting
  • SAP Consulting with Joule Demo

entry.title: Gamechanger Alert: Meet SAP Joule Studio for Real-World Impact
entry.content:
As someone who's still fairly new to SAP and learning about all it has to offer, I recently came across SAP Joule Studio—a tool that honestly surprised me. It’s part of SAP’s growing focus on AI, but instead of being just a tech buzzword, it’s something practical. Something helpful. Something that actually makes life easier.

So I thought I’d share what I’ve learned so far, for anyone else who’s curious like me.

 

What is SAP Joule Studio?

SAP Joule Studio is a low-code/no-code platform that lets you create AI-powered agents—little digital helpers that live inside your SAP system and support everyday tasks. These agents can:

  • Answer questions using real SAP data
  • Send reminders or alerts
  • Trigger workflows based on events
  • Help users navigate complex processes

The best part? You don’t need to be a developer to build one.

All you need is a good understanding of a business problem, and Joule Studio helps you turn that into a solution using easy visual tools.

 

Why This Is a Game-Changer (With Real Use Cases)

What really got me excited was seeing how real-world teams can use this tool to solve day-to-day challenges. Here are a few examples that really stood out to me:

✅HR & Employee Self-Service

  • Use Case: An AI agent that answers common HR questions like “How many vacation days do I have left?”, “What’s the process to update my bank details?”, or “When does my probation end?”
  • Impact: Reduces emails to HR, gives instant answers, and improves employee satisfaction.

✅Onboarding Assistant

  • Use Case: An AI agent that guides a new joiner through their first week—showing them how to complete paperwork, introducing them to teammates, and sending daily checklists.
  • Impact: Makes onboarding smoother, especially for remote hires or global teams.

✅Procurement Alerts

  • Use Case: A smart assistant that watches for delays from key suppliers and notifies procurement managers if a PO is stuck or overdue.
  • Impact: Keeps procurement responsive and improves relationships with vendors.

✅Project Management Summary

  • Use Case: A daily AI briefing that tells project leads about overdue tasks, timesheet gaps, or resource bottlenecks.
  • Impact: Saves hours of manual tracking and keeps teams aligned.

✅Compliance Checker

  • Use Case: A bot that checks which employees haven't completed their mandatory training or signed policy documents, and sends them a reminder.
  • Impact: Helps HR stay compliant without manual follow-ups.

✅IT Helpdesk Agent

  • Use Case: An agent that answers questions like “How do I reset my SAP password?” or “Where do I raise a ticket?”
  • Impact: Cuts down ticket volume for IT support and empowers users.

These aren’t just theoretical—they’re real issues we see in companies every day. And Joule Studio gives you a way to solve them quickly, without coding or waiting on IT.

 

Why Clients Will Love It Too

For clients, Joule Studio brings:

  • Self-service power to their SAP users
  • Less dependency on IT for small tasks
  • Smarter workflows with reminders, alerts, and guidance
  • Faster onboarding and training for new team members
  • Consistency across geographies and business units

AI becomes something helpful, not something scary or complex. It empowers users instead of overwhelming them.

 

Where You Can See It in Action

If you're like me and prefer learning visually, I highly recommend this official demo:

 SAP Joule Studio – AI for Everyone (YouTube)

You can also explore SAP’s official product page for more examples and how-to guides:
 SAP Joule Studio – Official Page

These are great starting points with visuals and real examples.

 

Getting Started with Joule Studio

If you’re ready to try it:

  1. Make sure your system is running on SAP BTP (Business Technology Platform).
  2. Go into SAP Build and access Joule Studio.
  3. Start by creating a small AI agent for a common task.
  4. Train it using real process examples and test in Preview mode.
  5. Roll it out with the right user permissions.

 

 

 

Remember—you don’t need to be technical. You just need to understand your business and what people are struggling with. Joule Studio helps you close that gap.

 

Final Thoughts (From One Learner to Another)

I’m still exploring SAP, still learning, and still asking questions. But tools like Joule Studio give me hope, not just for where tech is going, but for how we can make it work for people.

This isn’t about replacing humans—it’s about supporting them with small, smart helpers that remove friction and free up time.

If you're also exploring SAP tools and wondering where to start with AI, this might just be the right place. Try building a simple use case and see what it unlocks for your team.

And if you’ve tried it, I’d really love to hear what you’ve built. Let’s share and learn together, because the best ideas often come from the people closest to the problem.

 

#SAPJoule, #JouleStudio, #SAPBTP, #AIinSAP, #Gamechanger, #NoCodeAI, #SAPBuild, #EnterpriseAI, #DigitalTransformation, #SAPCommunity

entry.title: Joule based SAP HANA Application Migration Assistant - Transforming with Gen AI
entry.content:
In Early 2024, released first version of the SAP Business Application Studio extension which  Automates conversion of SAP HANA Database artifacts to SAP HANA Cloud artifacts.   SAP HANA  database objects (such as .hdbcds, .hdbtable, .hdbview, etc.) are transformed into their corresponding SAP CAP compliant database artifacts (e.g., .cds models).

The Q2-2025  release , enables service layer migration using GenAI capabilities in SAP Build Code. The assistant analyses SAP XS objects - xsodata ,xsjslib, xsjs and  files and generate corresponding service.cds, service.js and custom handler implementations under the CAP service layer. These AI capabilities  accelerates the migration of service layer while preserving structure and functional behaviour where possible. A sample conversion of xsodata to SAP CAP service definition is shown below. 

poWorklist.xsodata

service namespace "com.sap.openSAP.hana5.example.service"  { 
  "sap.hana.democontent.epm.models::PO_HEADER_EN" as "PO_WORKLIST" 
   keys ("PURCHASEORDERID","PURCHASEORDERITEM");
   
   "sap.hana.democontent.epm.models::PO_ITEM" as "PurchaseOrderItem" keys ("PURCHASEORDERID","PURCHASEORDERITEM");
   
   "PO.HeaderView" as "PurchaseOrderHeader" keys("PurchaseOrderId")
   navigates (     
        "PurchaseOrderHeader_PurchaseOrderItem" as "PurchaseOrderItems" from principal);

   association "PurchaseOrderHeader_PurchaseOrderItem"
      principal "PurchaseOrderHeader"("PurchaseOrderId") multiplicity "1"
      dependent "PurchaseOrderItem"("PurchaseOrderId") multiplicity "*";

}

service.cds

//converted from: /users/projects/hana-shine-xsa/core-xsjs/lib/xsodata/poWorklist.xsodata
service poWorklist @(path:'/poWorklist') {
    entity PO_WORKLIST as projection on SAP_HANA_DEMOCONTENT_EPM_MODELS_PO_HEADER_EN {
        *,
        PurchaseOrderItems: association to many PurchaseOrderItem on PurchaseOrderItems.PurchaseOrderId = $self.PURCHASEORDERID
    };

    entity PurchaseOrderItem as projection on SAP_HANA_DEMOCONTENT_EPM_MODELS_PO_ITEM;

    entity PurchaseOrderHeader as projection on PO.HeaderView {
        *,
        PurchaseOrderItems: association to many PurchaseOrderItem on PurchaseOrderItems.PurchaseOrderId = $self.PurchaseOrderId
    };
}

The converted code includes comments linking back to the original source files - making it easy for users to verify that key business logic has been accurately transformed.  In the similar way, all xsjslib and xsjs files are re-written as SAP CAP custom handlers. The resulting  modularised SAP CAP compliant project mirrors the original directory structure, ensuring familiarity and easy traceability.  All SAP HANA objects  will be adjusted automatically to follow SAP HANA Cloud naming conventions.

Additionally, the assistant generates a detailed report outlining recommended post-migration steps to guide users through final adjustments

As generative AI conversion is not guaranteed to be 100% accurate, human intervention is required post-migration to validate, refine, and productionize the application logic and service definitions.  

References

  • For prerequisites and step by step guide refer to corresponding xsc-to-cap  and xsa-to-cap repositories. 
  • Help Document: Migrating XS Classic Applications to SAP CAP
  • Help Document: SAP HANA XS Advanced Migration Guide

entry.title: Hiring Agents to reinforce your P2P team with SAP: When smart AI Agents become your new colleagues
entry.content:
Can you imagine an augmented team, working like a dream team, to help you negotiate, evaluate, and award an RFQ with your Vendors?  We're actively experimenting with this approach to bring the vision to reality for our clients.

Let’s start simple

We assume that today Personas are primarily working in a traditional manner, performing repetitive and time-consuming manual tasks across the various steps of the Procure-to-Pay (P2P) business process.

As an example, we can represent a portion of the P2P process like this:

People collaborate and communicate across departments and with their partner ecosystems, interacting with both SAP and non-SAP backend systems to gather the inputs needed to proceed with their tasks. These interactions are dynamic and iterative, driven by continuously evolving daily business needs.

Let’s assume we can now move a few boxes - representing the current 'as-is' process steps - from being 100% manual to something more advanced. This could involve elements from previous RPA investments, innovations from the new paradigm of Generative AI, or other emerging solutions. We can represent it like this:

What are we aiming for?

From RFQ creation to awarding and document generation, we envision a flow where different technologies can help in achieving:

  • Smart RFQ Creation: where e.g. SAP Joule Agent will suggest the most suitable Vendors. No panic, humans will make the final call.
  • Automated Response Processing: RPA e.g. from UiPath, and document recognition will handle heavy lifting.
  • Collaborative Evaluation: Agents based on different technologies (e.g., MyWave, Azure, others), and operating across departments such as Procurement, Production, and Finance, will analyze pricing and conditions, and evaluate related risks - working together in an Agent-to-Agent concerto.
  • Awarding Proposal: Gen-AI will suggest optimal awarding scenarios. You’ll validate them.
  • Document Automation: From award letters to purchase orders, everything will flow seamlessly.

Breaking silos, building a modern collaboration

With this flexible and scalable hyper-automated approach, we aim to break down traditional departmental silos. Information will flow seamlessly, decisions will be more informed, and response times will be drastically reduced. No worries about if colleagues are enjoying the beach during the summer or they are too busy to provide input - your needs will still be met.

Elevate Humans to focus on higher-value tasks

We are experimenting with Agents, autonomous or semi-autonomous, as new colleagues trained in the procurement business segment - such as in the automotive industry. These Agents support humans by using this solution to detect risks (e.g., identifying suppliers in conflict zones or with questionable labor practices), compare prices against market trends, and ensure production is not delayed due to logistics issues.

So, are you ready to hire your future digital colleagues?
I know you might be from the Order-to-Cash business process, right? This is a representation of the Procure-to-Pay process, but we're scaling across all business processes 😉

#EnjoyAI
